key_insight: A convex quasilinearization strategy turns nonlinear PDE solving into a sequence of linear least-squares problems with physics-informed neural network trial spaces that are linear in their trainable parameters.
engineering_relevance: This can improve reliability and efficiency for engineering PDE solvers by avoiding nonconvex gradient training while preserving high accuracy on challenging nonlinear flow and elasticity problems.
method: Bellman-Kalaba quasilinearization linearizes the nonlinear PDE into successive subproblems.
Each subproblem is discretized by collocation and solved via direct QR-based least squares.
Uses Linear-in-Learnables (LiL) PINN trial spaces such as random features, spectral polynomials, and trigonometric bases.
evidence_signal: The method was tested on seven benchmarks, including Bratu, Burgers, Buckley-Leverett, elasticity, Navier-Stokes in 2D/3D, and Darcy flow, and typically converged in single-digit outer iterations with strong parameter efficiency.
watch_item: Convergence is local and depends on a smallness condition, so performance may degrade if the initial guess or nonlinearity is too strong.
paper_title: A Convex Quasilinearization Method for Solving Nonlinear PDEs with Physics-Informed Neural Networks
